description: UE5 movement: CharacterMovementComponent deep-dive, custom movement modes, FloatingPawnMovement, ProjectileMovement, and flying/swimming

entries.title: CharacterMovementComponent — Deep Dive
entries.description: UCharacterMovementComponent is the most complex and powerful movement component in UE5. It handles walking, jumping, falling, flying, swimming, crouching, and network prediction out of the box.
entries.whatItIs: UCharacterMovementComponent is a specialized movement component for ACharacter that provides ground movement with gravity, jumping, air control, swimming, flying, crouching, and full client-side prediction for multiplayer. It is the most feature-complete movement solution in UE5.
whenToUse: Any humanoid character that needs walking, jumping, falling, and gravity
Multiplayer games that need client-side movement prediction and server correction
Characters that need swimming, flying, or crouching without custom implementation
When you need built-in slope handling, step-up, and floor detection
whenNotToUse: Vehicles — use UWheeledVehicleMovementComponent or custom physics
Top-down characters that don't need gravity or jumping — UFloatingPawnMovement is simpler
Fully physics-driven actors — use SetSimulatePhysics directly
commonMistakes: Setting MaxWalkSpeed in BeginPlay but it gets overridden by the Blueprint default — set it in the constructor or use a variable
Not understanding movement modes — Walking, Falling, Swimming, Flying, Custom are mutually exclusive states
Calling AddMovementInput without a controller possessing the pawn — movement input requires a controller
Modifying velocity directly instead of using AddForce/AddImpulse or LaunchCharacter — direct modification breaks prediction
Forgetting that JumpZVelocity is the initial launch velocity, not continuous upward force
Not overriding GetMaxSpeed() when adding custom movement modes — AI pathfinding uses this to estimate travel time
keyPoints: MaxWalkSpeed, MaxFlySpeed, MaxSwimSpeed: speed caps per movement mode
JumpZVelocity: initial vertical speed when jumping. AirControl: how much the player can steer mid-air (0-1).
GravityScale: multiplier on world gravity. Set to 0 for zero-G.
bOrientRotationToMovement: character rotates to face movement direction (third-person). RotationRate controls speed.
bUseControllerDesiredRotation: character rotates to face controller direction (first-person style).
LaunchCharacter(Velocity, bXYOverride, bZOverride): launch the character with an impulse (jump pads, explosions).
SetMovementMode(EMovementMode): switch between Walking, Flying, Swimming, Custom.
Custom movement: SetMovementMode(MOVE_Custom, CustomMode) + override PhysCustom() for your own physics.
Crouch(): built-in crouching with capsule resize. bCanCrouch must be true.
NavMovement: AI uses this component for pathfinding via MoveToLocation/MoveToActor.

entries.title: Projectile & Floating Movement Components
entries.description: UProjectileMovementComponent handles ballistic movement for projectiles. UFloatingPawnMovement provides simple gravity-free movement for top-down or flying pawns.
entries.whatItIs: UProjectileMovementComponent simulates projectile physics (initial speed, gravity, homing, bouncing) without full physics simulation. UFloatingPawnMovement provides basic acceleration-based movement without gravity for simple pawns like top-down characters or spectator cameras.
whenToUse: ProjectileMovement: bullets, rockets, grenades, thrown objects — anything with an initial velocity and optional gravity
ProjectileMovement: homing missiles that track a target
FloatingPawnMovement: top-down characters, RTS units, spectator cameras, simple AI pawns
FloatingPawnMovement: any pawn that doesn't need gravity, jumping, or complex ground detection
whenNotToUse: ProjectileMovement: for physics-driven objects that need to interact with the physics world (bouncing off dynamic objects) — use SetSimulatePhysics
FloatingPawnMovement: for characters that need gravity, jumping, or crouching — use CharacterMovementComponent
Hitscan weapons: don't need a projectile component at all — use a line trace
commonMistakes: Not setting InitialSpeed on ProjectileMovement — the projectile spawns and doesn't move
Forgetting that ProjectileMovement needs a root component with collision — without collision the projectile passes through everything
Setting bShouldBounce without an OnProjectileBounce handler — the projectile bounces forever
Using ProjectileMovement for hitscan weapons — just use a line trace, it's instant and cheaper
Not setting MaxSpeed on FloatingPawnMovement — the pawn accelerates indefinitely
keyPoints: ProjectileMovement: InitialSpeed, MaxSpeed, Gravity (ProjectileGravityScale), bShouldBounce, Bounciness
OnProjectileStop: delegate when the projectile hits and stops. OnProjectileBounce: on each bounce.
Velocity: read/set via Velocity property. SetVelocityInLocalSpace() for relative directions.
AddInputVector(): the standard way to move a FloatingPawn — call from your Pawn's movement input handler
